slides.title: Suno AI Music Demo
bullets: Show Suno AI music generation live
"For when you need a break from spreadsheets"
Demo how easy it is to create music with AI — fun cold open energy
slides.notes: Cold open moment: actually demo Suno to generate music live. The vibe is 'finance people need a break from spreadsheets sometimes' — light, fun, sets the tone for the episode.

slides.title: Stacks Raises $23M for Autonomous Close Operations
slides.notes: London-based Series A — AI agents for month-end close, reconciliations, journal entries, variance analysis. Targeting $100B+ legacy CFO software market.
slides.details:
Source: SiliconANGLE, Feb 19

Stacks secured Series A funding to automate monthly close, reconciliations, journal entries, and variance analysis. Their AI agents integrate directly with ERPs to handle the "two most manual workflows" in finance: accounting and month-end close.

Why it matters: We've discussed agentics extensively — this funding shows investors believe autonomous finance workflows are production-ready. The focus on month-end close specifically addresses our biggest pain point. The $100B+ legacy "office of the CFO" software market is wide open for disruption.

slides.title: Is This the Future of Every Company?
slides.notes: Zack Shapiro tweet — raises the question: is this what the future of every company looks like? Big implications for how businesses are structured and operated going forward.

slides.title: FloQast Adds Agent Builder for Accounting
slides.notes: FloQast launched an agent builder letting accounting teams build and deploy AI agents — includes a live demo. Big move from an established accounting platform into agentic workflows.

slides.title: Here's What Happened in AI in February
slides.notes: LinkedIn roundup by Alyona Mysko summarizing the biggest AI developments from February 2026. Great overview post to reference for the show.

slides.title: Every Company Will Have an Internal Agent Builder
slides.notes: Key prediction: 'Within a year, every company over 50 people will have at least one person whose full-time job is building internal agents.' Huge implications for finance & accounting teams — the rise of the internal agent builder role.

slides.title: AI Tax Return Accuracy Benchmark — GPT-5.4 Leads at 57%
slides.notes: LinkedIn post showing the % of tax returns each model completed PERFECTLY. GPT-5.4: 56.86%, Claude Opus 4.6: 52.94%, Gemini 3.1 Pro: 49.02%, GPT-5 w/ Web Search: 41.67%, GPT-5.2 Pro: 41.18%, Claude Sonnet 4.6: 37.25%, Gemini 3 Pro: 36.27%, Claude Opus 4.5: 36.27%, GPT-5.2: 33.82%, Gemini 2.5 Pro: 32.35%. The rivalry is heating up — but even the best model only nails ~57% perfectly. What does that mean for tax pros?

slides.title: Mount Rushmore of AI Apps
bullets: Each host picks their Mount Rushmore — the 4 AI apps that belong on the mountain
Joe's Mount Rushmore picks
Ore's Mount Rushmore picks
Debate: who has the better Mt. Rushmore?
slides.notes: Fun segment — Joe and Ore each reveal their Mount Rushmore of AI apps (top 4). Compare picks, debate who got it right. Great audience engagement potential.

slides.title: Joe's Take: SaaS is Not Dead
bullets: SaaS isn't dying — it's evolving
AI doesn't replace SaaS, it makes SaaS better
Every major SaaS platform is adding AI agents, not getting replaced by them
The subscription model still works — usage-based pricing is just a pricing change, not a death sentence
Enterprise buyers still want platforms, not a pile of AI scripts
slides.notes: Joe's argument: the 'SaaS is dead' narrative is overblown. AI is a feature layer on top of SaaS, not a replacement. Companies still need structured platforms with security, compliance, audit trails, and team collaboration — things agents alone don't solve.

slides.title: Ore's Take: Agent Platforms Everywhere
bullets: Every company will have an internal agent platform within 2 years
FloQast, Notion, Replit — everyone is shipping agent builders
The new hire isn't a person, it's an agent that runs 24/7
Finance teams will manage agent fleets the way they manage headcount
The question isn't IF agents are coming — it's who builds them first
slides.notes: Ore's argument: we're entering the age of agent platforms. It's not about one AI tool — it's about platforms that let you build, deploy, and manage agents across your entire operation. Finance teams that build agents now will have a massive advantage.
